attorneys.biography:
Cassie Murphy is a Partner at the Law Offices of Paone, Zaleski & Murphy, where she focuses her practice on divorce, child custody, and family law. She is one of fewer than 200 attorneys in New Jersey designated as a Certified Matrimonial Law Attorney by the New Jersey Supreme Court — a distinction requiring demonstrated experience, a rigorous examination, and peer recognition. Ms. Murphy has also been appointed by the Supreme Court of New Jersey to the Matrimonial Law Certification Committee for 2025 and 2026. She is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, one of only approximately 60 family law attorneys in New Jersey selected to the nation's most prestigious matrimonial law bar association. She is admitted to practice in New Jersey, New York, and the District of Columbia.

Ms. Murphy is an active leader in the New Jersey legal community, serving as Vice Chair of the Family Law Executive Committee of the New Jersey State Bar Association for the 2026–2027 term and as an appointed member of both the NJSBA Amicus Committee and the Monmouth County Family Law Committee. She is an Associate Editor of New Jersey Family Lawyer and co-authored the second edition of "Divorce in New Jersey." Ms. Murphy has been selected to the Super Lawyers list every year from 2020 to 2026, named to the Top 50 Women Super Lawyers in New Jersey for 2025 and 2026, and included in The Best Lawyers in America since 2025. She received the Martin Goldin Award for excellence in family law in 2018. Ms. Murphy earned her Bachelor of Arts from the University of Maryland, graduating summa cum laude, and her Juris Doctor from the University of Maryland School of Law, graduating cum laude.
attorneys.meta_description: Cassie Murphy, NJ Certified Matrimonial Attorney and AAML Fellow, handles complex divorce and custody cases in Red Bank and Monmouth County.

attorneys.biography:
Megan S. Murray is the founder of The Family Law Offices of Megan S. Murray in Holmdel, New Jersey, where she devotes her practice exclusively to family law matters including divorce, alimony, child support, equitable distribution, child custody, and high-net-worth cases. She is Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Certified Matrimonial Law Attorney and is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, where she serves on the Board of Managers. Megan is also a member of the Matrimonial Lawyers Alliance, an organization limited to 50 family law attorneys in New Jersey.

Megan has earned widespread recognition among New Jersey's top family law attorneys, earning Super Lawyers distinction from 2019 through 2026 and selection to Best Lawyers in America beginning in 2019. She was named a New Leader of the Bar by the New Jersey Law Journal in 2015 and received the Martin Goldin Award for excellence in family law in 2012. Professionally, she has served as Chair of the Family Law Section of the New Jersey State Bar Association (2023-2024), Secretary of that section (2019-2020), and Associate Managing Editor of the New Jersey Family Lawyer. Megan earned her Bachelor of Arts, summa cum laude, from Boston University and her Juris Doctorate from Wake Forest University School of Law in 2005.
attorneys.meta_description: Megan Murray, AAML Fellow and founder of her Holmdel firm, has guided Monmouth County families through divorce and high-net-worth cases since 2005.

attorneys.biography:
Eric S. Solotoff is a partner at Fox Rothschild LLP in Morristown, New Jersey, where his practice concentrates exclusively on family law litigation, mediation, and arbitration. He is Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney and is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers. His caseload spans complex matrimonial actions involving business valuation and high-conflict disputes, custody and parenting time, alimony and child support, equitable distribution, prenuptial and postnuptial agreements, palimony and cohabitant rights, domestic violence, and appellate practice. He has been involved in several precedent-setting New Jersey family law decisions, including Gnall v. Gnall on alimony duration and Maeker v. Ross on palimony, and he also serves as a mediator and arbitrator for parties seeking alternative dispute resolution in divorce matters.

Solotoff is ranked Band 1 by Chambers and Partners for High-Net-Worth Family/Matrimonial law in New Jersey, has been named to The Best Lawyers in America for Family Law since 2019, and has appeared on the Super Lawyers list continuously since 2007. He was appointed by the New Jersey Supreme Court to the District XB Ethics Committee, which he chaired from 2010 to 2011, and he is active in the American Bar Association, the New Jersey State Bar Association, and the Essex and Morris County Bar Associations. He edits Fox Rothschild's New Jersey Family Law Blog. Solotoff earned his B.A. cum laude from the State University of New York at Albany in 1989, his J.D. from Widener University School of Law in 1992, and an LL.M. in Taxation from Widener in 1994.
attorneys.meta_description: Eric Solotoff, Chambers Band 1-ranked matrimonial attorney at Fox Rothschild in Morristown, has litigated precedent-setting NJ family law cases since 1992.

attorneys.biography:
Francesca O'Cathain is the managing partner of O'Cathain Law Group in Hackensack, New Jersey, where her practice is devoted to divorce, matrimonial, and family law. She is board-certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney — a credential held by only about 3% of New Jersey attorneys — and is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, one of roughly 45 New Jersey family lawyers admitted to that organization. Her practice concentrates on high-asset and high-income divorce cases that proceed through trial, contentious custody disputes, and divorces involving children with special needs; she is also known in the Bergen County law enforcement and education communities for handling cases involving the pension and retirement benefits unique to those professions. She is Of Counsel to the New York firm Berkman, Bottger, Newman & Schein.

O'Cathain has been selected to the Super Lawyers list for New Jersey Family Law from 2020 to 2026, following several years on the Super Lawyers Rising Stars list. She currently serves as Past President of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers' New Jersey Chapter, and is active in the New Jersey State Bar Association, the Bergen County Bar Association, and multiple Irish-American legal associations, including as a founding member and former treasurer of the Irish American Bar Association of New Jersey. She frequently lectures on family law topics involving children with special needs and has received an Award of Merit from the National Center for Missing and Exploited Children for her work on Hague Convention cases. O'Cathain earned her B.A. from Claremont McKenna College in 2001 and her J.D. from the University of Connecticut School of Law in 2004.
attorneys.meta_description: Francesca O'Cathain, NJ Certified Matrimonial Attorney in Hackensack, focuses on high-asset divorce and special-needs custody cases in Bergen County.

attorneys.biography:
Patricia M. Barbarito is a Senior Partner and member of the Board of Directors and Executive Committee at Einhorn, Barbarito, Frost, Botwinick, Nunn & Musmanno, PC in Parsippany, where she has practiced since 1980. Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Attorney and a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, she represents high-profile and high-net-worth individuals, including corporate executives, in complex divorces, prenuptial agreements, and asset division matters. She has been ranked in the Chambers High Net Worth guide for New Jersey Family/Matrimonial law (Band 2) every year since 2022 and holds an AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell.

Ms. Barbarito has been listed in The Best Lawyers in America since 2006 and named to the Super Lawyers list every year since 2005, including repeated inclusion on the New Jersey Top 50 Women list. She was named among the New York Times' Top Ten Leaders in Matrimonial and Divorce Law in Northern New Jersey and received the New Jersey State Bar Association's Saul A. Tischler Award for her contributions to matrimonial law. She has chaired the NJSBA's Family Law Section and served on its Executive Committee, and represented Morris County on the NJSBA's Judicial and Prosecutorial Appointment Committee. She earned her Bachelor of Arts, magna cum laude, and her Juris Doctor from Seton Hall University.
attorneys.meta_description: Patricia Barbarito is a Chambers-ranked, Certified Matrimonial Attorney handling high-net-worth divorce cases in Morris County, New Jersey.

attorneys.biography:
Charles C. Abut is Of Counsel at Sarno da Costa D’Aniello Maceri Webb LLC in Hackensack, where he concentrates his practice on complex divorce litigation, arbitration, and mediation. He is certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney and is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers. A court-accredited mediator through the New Jersey Administrative Office of the Courts, Mr. Abut has mediated and arbitrated hundreds of matrimonial and business disputes and has litigated numerous cases resulting in reported appellate decisions, including Piscopo v. Piscopo and National Westminster Bank v. Gilchinsky, which established New Jersey's “badges of fraud” precedent for fraudulent conveyance claims.

Mr. Abut has been recognized in The Best Lawyers in America since 2001, earning “Lawyer of the Year” honors for Family Law Mediation in Hackensack in 2025 and for Family Law in Hackensack in 2018 and 2024. He has been selected to the Super Lawyers list every year since 2005 and holds an AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell, the organization's highest peer-review distinction. A frequent lecturer for the New Jersey Institute for Continuing Legal Education and the New Jersey Judicial College, he has published articles in the New Jersey Law Journal and been quoted by The New York Times, The Wall Street Journal, and other national media. Mr. Abut earned his Bachelor of Arts from Columbia University, graduating cum laude, and his Juris Doctor from Cornell University Law School.
attorneys.meta_description: Charles Abut is a Certified Matrimonial Attorney and AAML Fellow handling high-conflict divorce and mediation cases in Bergen County, NJ.

attorneys.biography:
Robert J. Adinolfi is a founding shareholder and managing partner of Adinolfi, Roberto & Burick, P.A. in Mount Laurel, where he has practiced family law for nearly five decades. He is certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney, a distinction held by only a small fraction of the state's family law practitioners, and he handles the full range of matrimonial matters including divorce, custody, alimony, equitable distribution, prenuptial agreements, and complex tax issues arising from marital dissolution. He litigated the landmark “engagement ring” case, Aronow v. Silver, which established the concept of no-fault engagement in New Jersey.

A Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers since 1989, Mr. Adinolfi served as President of its New Jersey Chapter from 2014 to 2015 after holding every other office on its board. He has been selected to the Super Lawyers list every year since 2006 and has been recognized in The Best Lawyers in America for eight consecutive years. He is a longtime member of the Camden County Bar Association and serves on the Family Law Executive Committee of the New Jersey State Bar Association. Mr. Adinolfi earned his Bachelor of Science in Accounting, cum laude, from Saint Joseph's University and his Juris Doctor from Rutgers University School of Law in Camden.
attorneys.meta_description: Robert Adinolfi is a Certified Matrimonial Attorney and AAML Fellow with nearly 50 years handling divorce and custody cases in Camden County, NJ.

attorneys.biography:
Allison Ansell chairs the Matrimonial & Family Law Department at Ansell Grimm & Aaron, PC in Ocean, New Jersey, a group of six attorneys handling the full spectrum of divorce and family law matters. Following law school, she served as law clerk to the Honorable Alexander D. Lehrer, then the presiding judge of the Family Part of Monmouth County. She is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers and a member of the New Jersey State Bar Association's Family Law Executive Committee.

Ms. Ansell has been selected to the Super Lawyers list nearly every year since 2007 and was recognized in The Best Lawyers in America's 2026 edition. In 2019, she received the Bernard H. Hoffman Family Lawyer of the Year Award. She is active in the Monmouth County Bar Association, where she has served as a trustee and co-chaired the Family Law Committee, and previously served as President of Women Lawyers of Monmouth County and as a member of the Monmouth County District Ethics Committee. Ms. Ansell earned her Bachelor of Arts in Criminal Justice from American University and her Juris Doctor from Western New England College School of Law.
attorneys.meta_description: Allison Ansell chairs the family law department at Ansell Grimm & Aaron, handling divorce and custody matters throughout Ocean County, NJ.

attorneys.biography:
Mary Ann Bauer is a founding partner of Bauer & Karch Law Group, LLC in Lebanon, where she has practiced family law since graduating from Seton Hall University School of Law in 1988. She is a certified matrimonial attorney and a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, and she is also a trained collaborative law attorney, mediator, and certified arbitrator, giving clients multiple paths to resolve disputes outside of traditional litigation. Her practice covers divorce, custody and parenting time, alimony, child support, and domestic violence matters throughout Hunterdon County and the surrounding area.

Ms. Bauer has been selected to the Super Lawyers list every year since 2015 and has been repeatedly named to the Top 50 Women New Jersey Super Lawyers list. In 2014, she received the Hunterdon County Bar Association's Professional Lawyer of the Year Award. She also serves the family court system as an appointed parenting coordinator and mediator. Ms. Bauer earned her Bachelor of Science with honors from Rutgers University and her Juris Doctor, cum laude, from Seton Hall University School of Law.
attorneys.meta_description: Mary Ann Bauer is a Certified Matrimonial Attorney and AAML Fellow providing divorce and mediation services in Hunterdon County, NJ.

attorneys.biography:
Bruce Chase is the founder of Chase & Chase LLC in Hackensack, where he has practiced family law since 1978, following his admission to the New Jersey Bar to join his father's existing firm. He handles the full range of contested divorce matters, including child custody, shared residential custody, equitable distribution of businesses, alimony, and child support, and his background in real estate, business, and commercial leasing informs his approach to complex asset division. He is a Certified Matrimonial Law Attorney under the Supreme Court of New Jersey and a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, where he currently serves as First Vice President of the New Jersey Chapter.

Chase has been selected to the Super Lawyers list every year since 2007 and serves as chairperson of an early settlement panel that helps resolve contested divorce cases before trial. He is a past president of the Bergen County Bar Association and has co-chaired its Family Law Committee for more than a decade, in addition to serving on the New Jersey State Bar Association's Family Law Section Executive Committee since 1998. A frequent lecturer for the State Bar's continuing education programs, he has authored articles for the Bergen Barrister and the New Jersey Family Lawyer. Chase earned his Juris Doctor from Rutgers University School of Law in 1978 and his Bachelor of Arts, magna cum laude, from American University in 1975.
attorneys.meta_description: Bruce Chase, NJ Certified Matrimonial Attorney and AAML Fellow, has handled contested divorce and custody cases in Bergen County since 1978.

attorneys.biography:
Cary B. Cheifetz is Of Counsel in the Family Law Practice Group at A.Y. Strauss in Livingston, where his practice focuses on complex divorce and custody matters, high-net-worth marital dissolutions, and family law issues involving bankruptcy, professional malpractice, and jurisdictional challenges. He is frequently retained nationally as an expert witness, mediator, and arbitrator, and is ranked Band 1 by Chambers and Partners for High-Net-Worth Family/Matrimonial law in New Jersey. He is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers and a Diplomate of the American College of Family Trial Lawyers.

Cheifetz has been selected to the Super Lawyers list since 2005 and has been named to The Best Lawyers in America for Family Law since 2007. A past president of both the Essex County Bar Association and the New Jersey Chapter of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, he has also chaired the Family Law Executive Committees of the Essex County and New Jersey State Bar Associations. He is co-author of New Jersey Family Law (2nd Edition, LexisNexis) and has served on the faculty of the New Jersey Judicial College and the New Jersey Family Part Judges Retreat. Cheifetz earned his Juris Doctor from New York Law School and his Bachelor of Arts from George Washington University.
attorneys.meta_description: Cary Cheifetz, a Chambers Band 1 and AAML Fellow attorney in Livingston, handles high-net-worth divorce cases throughout Essex County, NJ.

attorneys.biography:
Alix Claps is a Partner at Heymann & Fletcher in Randolph, where she concentrates her practice on family law, including divorce, custody, and parenting time disputes. A native of Randolph, she clerked for the Honorable Edith K. Payne and the Honorable Christine A. Farrington in the Civil Division of Essex County Superior Court before joining the firm, and she is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers. She also represents victims of domestic violence pro bono through the Jersey Battered Women's Legal Advocacy Project.

Claps was named a New Jersey Rising Star by Super Lawyers from 2019 through 2023 and was selected to the Super Lawyers list in 2026. She is an active member of the New Jersey State Bar Association's Family Law Executive Committee, where she previously co-chaired the Family Law Section's Young Lawyers Subcommittee, and belongs to the Barry I. Croland Family Law American Inn of Court and the Morris County Bar Association. Before pursuing law, Claps worked professionally as a stage manager; she earned her Juris Doctor, cum laude, from Rutgers Law School in Newark, her Bachelor of Theatre Arts from the University of Michigan, and her Master of Arts from DePaul University.
attorneys.meta_description: Alix Claps, an AAML Fellow and Partner at Heymann & Fletcher, handles divorce and custody matters throughout Morris County, New Jersey.

attorneys.biography:
Amy Sara Cores is the founder of MadJam Music & Entertainment Law, LLC in Howell, where she handles family law matters alongside her entertainment law practice. Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney and a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, she is an experienced appellate attorney and has represented clients in cases before both the New Jersey Supreme Court and the Supreme Court of the United States. She is also recognized as a national expert on international child abduction, having worked with the U.S. State Department and the National Center for Missing and Exploited Children to secure the return of abducted children.

Cores has been selected to the Super Lawyers list since 2014, following selection to the Rising Stars list from 2008 through 2013, and received the New Jersey State Bar Association's Young Lawyer of the Year Award in 2009. She serves on the Amicus Committee of both the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers and its New Jersey Chapter, and is a member of the New Jersey State Bar Association, the Monmouth Bar Association, and the American Bar Association. Cores earned her Juris Doctor from Rutgers University School of Law in Camden and holds two graduate degrees, including a Master of Arts from Florida State University and a Master of Arts from the Berklee College of Music.
attorneys.meta_description: Amy Sara Cores, a Certified Matrimonial Attorney and AAML Fellow in Howell, handles family law and international custody cases in Monmouth County.

attorneys.biography:
Carolyn N. Daly chairs the Family Law Group at Cohen Seglias Pallas Greenhall & Furman PC in Newark, where she is certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney. Her practice spans divorce litigation, mediation, and arbitration, with particular emphasis on complex financial matters such as restricted stock units, deferred compensation, cryptocurrency, and closely held business interests, and family court judges regularly appoint her as a mediator, guardian ad litem, and parent coordinator. She is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers and a past president of its New Jersey Chapter.

Daly has been named to The Best Lawyers in America for Family Law, Family Law Arbitration, and Family Law Mediation since 2025, and has been selected to the Super Lawyers list every year since 2013, including repeated recognition on the Top 50 Women and Top 100 New Jersey lawyer lists. She argued before the Supreme Court of New Jersey in Cardali v. Cardali on behalf of AAML-NJ as amicus curiae, and serves on the New Jersey Courts' Supreme Court Family Practice Committee and the New Jersey State Bar Association's Family Law Executive Committee. Daly earned her Juris Doctor from Seton Hall University School of Law in 1996 and her Bachelor of Arts from the University of Notre Dame.
attorneys.meta_description: Carolyn Daly chairs the Family Law Group at Cohen Seglias, handling complex divorce and mediation matters in Newark and Essex County, NJ.

attorneys.biography:
Jerry S. D’Aniello is a partner and managing partner of the Somerset County office of Sarno da Costa D’Aniello Maceri Webb LLC, where he devotes his practice exclusively to divorce and family law, including complex, high-net-worth matrimonial matters. He is certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney and began his career as an Assistant Prosecutor in Somerset County before moving into private practice. A seasoned negotiator and trained mediator, arbitrator, and collaborative law attorney, he is among a limited number of New Jersey practitioners authorized to mediate cases involving active domestic violence restraining orders.

Mr. D’Aniello is a Fellow and past President of the New Jersey Chapter of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, a Diplomate of the American College of Family Trial Lawyers, and a member of the International Academy of Family Lawyers. He holds an AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell and has been recognized in Best Lawyers in America and the New Jersey Super Lawyers Top 100 list for multiple consecutive years. He earned his J.D. from Pace University School of Law and his B.S. from Northeastern University.
attorneys.meta_description: Jerry D’Aniello, a Certified Matrimonial Law Attorney and AAML Fellow, represents Somerset County families in high-asset divorce and custody matters.

attorneys.biography:
Richard S. Diamond is the founder and managing partner of Diamond & Diamond, P.A. in Short Hills, where his practice is devoted entirely to divorce and family law, including high-net-worth divorce, post-divorce litigation, and business valuation matters tied to matrimonial disputes. He is certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Trial Attorney, a distinction held by a small fraction of the state’s attorneys, and has practiced family law in New Jersey for four decades. He is also an accredited divorce and family law mediator and arbitrator, with specialized training to handle economic mediation in cases involving active domestic violence restraining orders.

Mr. Diamond is a member of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers and has served on the Union and Essex County Family Law Early Settlement Panel programs since the late 1980s. He has been recognized in Who’s Who in American Law and named to the New Jersey Super Lawyers list. He earned his J.D. from Seton Hall University School of Law and his B.A. in Economics and Business Administration from Rutgers University.
attorneys.meta_description: Richard Diamond, a Certified Matrimonial Trial Attorney, has represented Essex County clients in complex divorce and family law matters for four decades.

attorneys.biography:
Adam M. Eisenhut is a partner at Maleski, Eisenhut & Zielinski, LLC in Flemington, where he concentrates his practice on family law matters including divorce, custody, alimony, and post-judgment applications. He is certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney and is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers. Mr. Eisenhut is also a trained family law mediator and Collaborative Divorce attorney, and previously served as a court-approved mediator for Special Civil matters during his judicial clerkship.

Mr. Eisenhut is an active member of the Hunterdon County Bar Association, where he serves on the Family Law Practice and Collaborative Law Committees, and he served eight years on the Vicinage XIII Ethics Committee. He has been named a New Jersey Super Lawyer since 2019 after being recognized as a Rising Star in 2016 and 2017. He earned his J.D. from the University of Pittsburgh School of Law, where he received the CALI Award for Intellectual Property, and his B.A. cum laude from Boston University.
attorneys.meta_description: Adam Eisenhut, an AAML Fellow certified in matrimonial law, helps Hunterdon County families resolve divorce, custody, and support disputes.

attorneys.biography:
Jeffrey K. Epstein is a partner and General Counsel at Szaferman, Lakind, Blumstein & Blader, P.C. in Lawrenceville, focusing his practice on major matrimonial litigation, including divorce, custody, post-judgment proceedings, and prenuptial agreements. He is certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney and has maintained an AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell since 2003. Prior to joining the firm in 2004, he practiced for sixteen years at Wilentz, Goldman & Spitzer, where he co-chaired the family law department.

Mr. Epstein is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers and chairs the Supreme Court committee that reviews matrimonial law certification applications. He has served on the Executive Committee of the State Bar’s Family Law Section, is a blue ribbon panelist for the Mercer County Early Settlement Program, and has been named to New Jersey Super Lawyers for over twenty years and to Best Lawyers in America. He earned his J.D. from Touro College, Jacob D. Fuchsberg Law Center, and his B.A. from Lake Forest College.
attorneys.meta_description: Jeffrey Epstein, an AV Preeminent matrimonial attorney, has guided Mercer County families through divorce and complex litigation for nearly forty years.

attorneys.biography:
Christine C. Fitzgerald is a partner at SeidenFreed LLC, practicing family law from the firm’s Cranford office. She is certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney and is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, where she also serves on the Board of Managers of the New Jersey Chapter. Ms. Fitzgerald began her career clerking for the Honorable Thomas P. Zampino before entering private practice at several prominent family law firms across Bergen, Hudson, and Morris counties.

She currently serves as Chair-Elect of the Family Law Executive Committee of the New Jersey State Bar Association and as President of the Hudson County Bar Association, and she was named the 2018 Family Lawyer of the Year by the Hudson County Bar Association and Foundation. She has been included in New Jersey Super Lawyers since 2022 and in Best Lawyers in America since 2021. She earned her J.D. from New York Law School and her B.A. from Seton Hall University.
attorneys.meta_description: Christine Fitzgerald, a Certified Matrimonial Law Attorney, represents Union County clients in divorce, custody, and family law matters statewide.

attorneys.biography:
Jennifer Fortunato is a partner in the Family/Matrimonial Practice Group and a member of the Board of Directors at Einhorn, Barbarito, Frost, Botwinick, Nunn & Musmanno, PC, where she has practiced family law since 1993. She is certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Attorney and is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers. In addition to her family law practice, Ms. Fortunato has built a specialized Special Education Law practice, advocating for children with disabilities in IEP proceedings, due process hearings, and related litigation.

Ms. Fortunato serves on the New Jersey State Bar Association’s Family Law Executive Committee and was appointed by the Supreme Court of New Jersey to a four-year term on the District Ethics Committee. She holds an AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell and has been named to New Jersey Super Lawyers and Best Lawyers in America. She earned her J.D. from Wake Forest University School of Law and her B.S. cum laude from Boston University.
attorneys.meta_description: Jennifer Fortunato, an AV Preeminent matrimonial attorney, represents Morris County families in divorce, custody, and special education matters.

attorneys.biography:
Bonnie C. Frost is a partner at Einhorn, Barbarito, Frost, Botwinick, Nunn & Musmanno, PC in Parsippany, where she has practiced family law since 1985 with a particular focus on appeals of family law matters. She is certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney and is frequently retained as an expert witness or ethics consultant for attorneys facing professional conduct issues, in addition to handling private mediation and arbitration.

Ms. Frost is a Fellow and former Secretary of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers and has chaired the New Jersey State Bar Association's Appellate Practice Committee since 2010, after previously serving on the Disciplinary Review Board for 13 years, including as its chair. She holds an AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell and has been named to the New Jersey Super Lawyers list every year since 2005, in addition to the New Jersey Law Journal's 2022 Lifetime Achievement Award. She earned her J.D. from Seton Hall University School of Law and her B.A. from Douglass College at Rutgers University.
attorneys.meta_description: Bonnie Frost, a Certified Matrimonial Law Attorney and AAML Fellow, has represented Morris County families in appellate and family law matters since 1985.

attorneys.biography:
Sean P. Gaynor is a partner at Cohen Seglias Pallas Greenhall & Furman PC in Newark, where he is a New Jersey Supreme Court Certified Matrimonial Law Attorney devoted to representing family law clients across custody, alimony, child support, and complex asset division matters, including business interests, stock options, and cryptocurrency. Before entering private family law practice, he served as an assistant prosecutor with the Morris County Prosecutor's Office, handling a broad caseload of criminal matters including domestic violence and firearms offenses, and later clerked for the Hon. Ann R. Bartlett in Warren County's family division.

Mr. Gaynor is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers and a board member and volunteer attorney for the Battered Women's Legal Advocacy Project. He has been named to the New Jersey Super Lawyers Rising Stars list every year since 2022 and is a member of the New Jersey State Bar Association and Morris County Bar Association Family Law Sections. He earned his J.D. from the Catholic University of America Columbus School of Law and his B.A. from the same institution.
attorneys.meta_description: Sean Gaynor, a Certified Matrimonial Law Attorney and AAML Fellow, represents Essex County families in custody, alimony, and complex divorce matters.

attorneys.biography:
Richard F. Iglar is a partner at Skoloff & Wolfe, P.C. in Livingston, where he has practiced family and matrimonial law exclusively since 1999. He is certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney and handles complex divorce, custody, and high-net-worth matrimonial disputes involving business valuations and sophisticated income structures, with trial and appellate experience before New Jersey state and federal courts, including the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit.

Mr. Iglar is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers and served as President of its New Jersey Chapter, in addition to sitting on the chapter's Board of Managers since 2009. He is ranked Band 3 in the Chambers High Net Worth Guide for Family/Matrimonial Law and has been named to the New Jersey Super Lawyers list every year since 2010. He earned his J.D. from Boston College Law School and his B.A., cum laude and Phi Beta Kappa, from Rutgers College.
attorneys.meta_description: Richard Iglar, a Chambers-ranked Certified Matrimonial Law Attorney, represents Essex County clients in high-net-worth divorce and custody matters.

attorneys.biography:
Robert B. Kornitzer is the founder and managing lawyer of Kornitzer Family Law, LLC, with offices in Paramus and Totowa, where he has practiced family law throughout North Jersey since 1992. He is certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney and, as a dual JD/MBA holder from Boston University, brings particular expertise in the financial aspects of divorce, including business valuations and asset protection, along with experience representing clients through religious (Jewish) divorce proceedings and Rabbinical Courts in New Jersey and New York.

Mr. Kornitzer is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers and a member of the American Academy of Certified Financial Litigators. He has been ranked in Best Lawyers every year since 2016 and named to the New Jersey Super Lawyers list since 2019, and he serves as a Trustee of the Passaic County Bar Association and a Matrimonial Early Settlement Panelist in Passaic and Bergen counties. He earned his J.D. and M.B.A. from Boston University and his B.S. from the State University of New York at Albany.
attorneys.meta_description: Robert Kornitzer, a Certified Matrimonial Attorney and AAML Fellow, guides Bergen and Passaic County clients through divorce and financial settlements.

attorneys.biography:
Scott A. Laterra is a founding partner of Laterra & Hodge, LLC in Hackensack, where he has practiced family law since 1999. He is a Certified Matrimonial Trial Attorney, a distinction held by only about two percent of New Jersey attorneys, and handles divorce, custody, and family law matters throughout Bergen County, with appellate experience in cases such as Bove v. Bove and Wichard v. Wichard.

Mr. Laterra is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers and a member of the Bergen County Bar Association and New Jersey State Bar Association. He received Avvo's Client's Choice Award in 2012, 2013, and 2015, and was recognized by the New Jersey State Bar Association for his contribution to the amicus curiae brief in Lewis v. Harris. He earned his J.D. from Seton Hall University School of Law and his B.A. from Rider University.
attorneys.meta_description: Scott Laterra, a Certified Matrimonial Trial Attorney and AAML Fellow, represents Bergen County clients in divorce and custody matters.

attorneys.biography:
Jeralyn L. Lawrence is the founder and Managing Member of Lawrence Law, with offices in Watchung and Red Bank, where she devotes her practice exclusively to matrimonial, divorce, and family law, including high-net-worth cases involving celebrities, professional athletes, and other high-profile clients. She is certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney and has argued before the New Jersey Supreme Court on behalf of the NJSBA and the AAML/NJ on matters including alimony modification and child protection standards.

Ms. Lawrence is a Past President of the New Jersey State Bar Association, the AAML New Jersey Chapter, and the Somerset County Bar Association, and she chaired the NJSBA's Family Law Section during the 2014 passage of New Jersey's alimony reform statute. She has held the Martindale-Hubbell AV Preeminent rating since 2009, been named to the New Jersey Super Lawyers list every year since 2007, and been recognized as a Best Lawyers 'Lawyer of the Year' on multiple occasions since first being listed in 2015. She earned her B.A. from Kean University and her J.D., summa cum laude, from Seton Hall University School of Law.
attorneys.meta_description: Jeralyn Lawrence, a Past NJSBA President and AV Preeminent Attorney, represents Somerset and Monmouth County families in complex divorce matters.

attorneys.biography:
Ronald G. Lieberman is a partner/shareholder at Rigden, Lieberman & Mignogna, P.A., with offices in Marlton and Moorestown, where he has practiced family and matrimonial law for over 25 years, including divorce, custody, alimony, equitable distribution, and appellate matters before the Appellate Division. He has been certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney since 2010, a distinction held by only about 3% of the state's attorneys, and regularly appears before the Family Part in Burlington, Camden, Atlantic, Cumberland, and Gloucester counties.

Mr. Lieberman is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers and chaired the New Jersey State Bar Association's Family Law Section from 2020 to 2021, leading more than 2,500 family law practitioners statewide; he is also a past President of the Camden County Bar Association and its Bar Foundation. He has held the Martindale-Hubbell AV Preeminent rating and been named to the New Jersey Super Lawyers list every year since 2014, in addition to being recognized as a Best Lawyers 'Lawyer of the Year' for Family Law in Cherry Hill. He earned his B.A. from the University of Delaware and his J.D. from New York Law School.
attorneys.meta_description: Ronald Lieberman, an AV-rated Certified Matrimonial Attorney, represents Burlington and Camden County clients in high-asset divorce matters.

attorneys.biography:
Frank A. Louis is a partner in the Family Law Department at Greenbaum, Rowe, Smith & Davis LLP in Red Bank, where he has practiced family law for over 45 years, concentrating on negotiating and litigating economically complex divorce cases. He was the founding partner of Louis & Russell, a boutique matrimonial firm in Toms River, and is co-author of the treatise New Jersey Family Law Volume II: Divorce, Alimony & Property Division, published by Gann Law Books.

Mr. Louis is a Fellow of both the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers and the American College of Family Trial Lawyers, and he is an Emeritus member and past Chair of the New Jersey State Bar Association's Family Law Section Executive Committee, as well as a past President of the Ocean County Bar Association. He has held the Martindale-Hubbell AV Preeminent rating and has been listed in Best Lawyers in America for Family Law since 1993, in addition to being named to the Lawdragon 500 Leading Family Lawyers list. He earned his J.D. from Rutgers University School of Law and his B.A., cum laude, from Brooklyn College of the City University of New York.
attorneys.meta_description: Frank Louis, an AV Preeminent Family Law attorney with over 45 years' experience, represents Monmouth and Ocean County clients in complex divorce matters.

attorneys.biography:
Madeline Marzano-Lesnevich is Of Counsel in the Family Law Department of O'Cathain Law Group in Hackensack, where she focuses on complex matrimonial matters involving high-net-worth individuals, business owners, and professional athletes, along with high-conflict custody and relocation disputes. Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney, she is a Diplomate of the American College of Family Trial Lawyers — one of only six in New Jersey and the first woman in the state to hold the distinction — and carries an AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell. A former President of both the national American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers and its New Jersey Chapter, Marzano-Lesnevich has also chaired the Family Law Section of the New Jersey State Bar Association and serves as a Fellow of the International Academy of Family Lawyers and the Litigation Counsel of America.

Her honors include the AAML New Jersey Chapter's Fellow of the Year Award and the New Jersey State Bar Association Family Law Section's Saul R. Tischler Award for lifetime achievement. She earned her J.D. from Rutgers Law School, an M.A. from the City University of New York, and a B.S. from Columbia University.
attorneys.meta_description: Diplomate-level NJ matrimonial attorney serving Hackensack and Bergen County families through complex, high-net-worth divorce and custody matters.

attorneys.biography:
Christopher Musulin is the founding partner of Musulin Groves & Greenfield (formerly Musulin Law Firm) in Mount Holly, with a second office in Marlton, where for over 35 years he has practiced as both a divorce and family law litigator and a divorce and family law mediator. A Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers since 2006 and an AAML-certified arbitrator since 2010, he is also an Accredited Professional Mediator through the New Jersey Association of Professional Mediators and holds an AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell.

Musulin has served the Burlington County and New Jersey State Bar Associations extensively, including as longtime Chair or Co-Chair of the Burlington County Bar Association's Family Law Bench/Bar Committee and as a member of the NJSBA Family Law Executive Committee and the New Jersey Supreme Court's Family Practice Committee. He is a frequent author and speaker on family law topics for the New Jersey Family Lawyer and NJICLE, and was honored with the Burlington County Bar Association's 2024 Martin L. Haines Award. He earned his J.D. from Rutgers School of Law–Camden and his B.A., with distinction, from Rutgers University.
attorneys.meta_description: Burlington County divorce attorney and AAML Fellow representing Mount Holly and Marlton clients in complex family law litigation and mediation.

attorneys.biography:
Matheu D. Nunn is the Managing Partner of Einhorn, Barbarito, Frost, Botwinick, Nunn & Musmanno, PC in Parsippany, New Jersey, where he co-chairs the firm's Family/Matrimonial Practice and its General Appellate Practice. Certified by the New Jersey Supreme Court as a Matrimonial Attorney and a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, Mat represents clients across the full range of divorce and family law matters, including athletes, executives, business owners, and other high-profile professionals. A former county and municipal prosecutor, he is an accomplished litigator who has argued more than 100 appellate and Supreme Court matters, including precedent-setting family law decisions such as Cardali v. Cardali and Bisbing v. Bisbing. Mat is AV Preeminent rated by Martindale-Hubbell and has been named to Best Lawyers in America for Family Law since 2019 and to the New Jersey Super Lawyers list since 2022.

His peer recognition includes three New Jersey Law Journal “Attorney of the Year” finalist honors and the Essex County Bar Association's Family Practitioner of the Year award. He serves on the New Jersey State Bar Association's Family Law Executive Committee and Amicus Committee and is a former trustee of the Morris County Bar Association. He earned his J.D. with honors from Rutgers School of Law and his B.A. from the University of Delaware.
attorneys.meta_description: Matheu Nunn is a Board-certified NJ Matrimonial Attorney and AAML Fellow leading complex divorce litigation from Morris County, New Jersey.

attorneys.biography:
John P. Paone, Jr. is the founder and senior partner of the Law Offices of Paone, Zaleski & Murphy, with offices in Woodbridge and Red Bank, New Jersey. A Certified Matrimonial Law Attorney with more than 40 years of experience in divorce, custody, and family law matters, Mr. Paone is a Diplomate of the American College of Family Trial Lawyers — an elite group limited to the top 100 divorce attorneys in the United States — and a Fellow and former President of the New Jersey Chapter of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers. He is rated AV Preeminent by Martindale-Hubbell, the organization's highest peer-review distinction. 

Mr. Paone has been named a Best Lawyers “Lawyer of the Year” for Family Law three times (2015, 2020, and 2024) and has been listed in Super Lawyers every year since 2005. He has held numerous leadership roles in the New Jersey legal community, including Chair of the New Jersey Supreme Court's Board on Attorney Certification, Past President of the Middlesex County Bar Association, and Past Chair of the New Jersey State Bar Association's Family Law Section. He earned his J.D. from American University's Washington College of Law and his B.A. from Rutgers University.
attorneys.meta_description: John P. Paone Jr. is an AV-rated, AAML Fellow matrimonial attorney who has served Middlesex and Monmouth County, NJ divorce clients since 1982.

attorneys.biography:
Peter C. Paras is Counsel at Ansell Grimm & Aaron, PC in Ocean, New Jersey, where he devotes his practice exclusively to matrimonial and family law. Licensed in New Jersey since 1976, he is a Certified Matrimonial Law Attorney and a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers. He holds Martindale-Hubbell's AV Preeminent rating, has been named a New Jersey Super Lawyer every year since the award's inception, and has been recognized in Best Lawyers in America since 2015.

Peter is an active member of the New Jersey State Bar Association's Family Law Section, having served for many years on its Executive Committee, and has served on the Monmouth Bar Association's Family Law Committee for more than three decades. He is a member of the Matrimonial Lawyers Alliance, a highly selective group limited to fifty family law attorneys, and serves as a Master in the Ocean-Monmouth Family Law American Inn of Court. He earned his J.D. from Villanova University School of Law in 1976 and his B.A. from the University of Pennsylvania in 1973.
attorneys.meta_description: Peter Paras, AV-rated Monmouth County divorce attorney and AAML Fellow, has guided New Jersey families through matrimonial matters since 1976.

attorneys.biography:
Jean Ramatowski is Of Counsel at Rozin | Golinder Law, LLC, practicing from the firm's East Brunswick, New Jersey office. Licensed in New Jersey since 1983, she is a Certified Matrimonial Law Attorney, a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, and an AAML-approved Arbitrator. She holds Martindale-Hubbell's AV Preeminent rating and has been named a New Jersey Super Lawyer every year since 2005. Jean concentrates her practice on divorce and family law, including custody, support, equitable distribution, and pre- and post-nuptial agreements.

Jean has held numerous leadership roles in the New Jersey legal community, including chairing the Supreme Court's District VII Ethics Committee (1996–1997) and chairing the Board of Trustees of the New Jersey Lawyers' Fund for Client Protection (2005–2012). She is a qualified Family Law Mediator through the Institute for Dispute Resolution of New Jersey and was recognized as a Master of the Bench by the American Inns of Court Foundation. She earned her B.A. in Economics from Rutgers University in 1980 and her J.D. from Seton Hall University School of Law in 1983.
attorneys.meta_description: Since 1983, Jean Ramatowski has handled divorce, custody, and support matters for families throughout Middlesex County, New Jersey.

attorneys.biography:
Bonnie M.S. Reiss is a family law attorney with Lomurro Law in Freehold, New Jersey, where she focuses on divorce mediation, family law arbitration, and the resolution of complex matrimonial disputes. A Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers and NJ Supreme Court-certified divorce and family law mediator, she has spent more than four decades litigating and mediating custody disputes, business valuations, and other contested matrimonial matters throughout central New Jersey. She holds Martindale-Hubbell's AV Preeminent rating, the organization's highest distinction for legal ability and ethical standards. 

Reiss has been named to Super Lawyers in New Jersey for family law and alternative dispute resolution numerous times since the list's 2005 inception. She is active in bar leadership, serving on the Executive Committee of the New Jersey State Bar Association's Family Law Section and on the New Jersey Supreme Court's Family Practice Committee, and has previously served the Middlesex County Bar Association as trustee. Reiss earned her J.D. from Rutgers University School of Law in 1978 and her B.A. from Rutgers University in 1975, and began her career clerking for a Family Court judge before entering private practice.
attorneys.meta_description: Bonnie M.S. Reiss is an AV Preeminent-rated divorce mediator and AAML Fellow serving Freehold and Monmouth County, NJ families since 1978.

attorneys.biography:
Sheryl J. Seiden is a founding partner of SeidenFreed LLC in Cranford, New Jersey, where she focuses exclusively on family law, including complex divorce litigation, custody, and prenuptial agreements. Licensed in New Jersey since 1996, she is a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers' New Jersey Chapter and its immediate past president, and she holds Martindale-Hubbell's AV Preeminent rating, the organization's highest distinction for legal ability and ethics. She is also a qualified mediator under New Jersey Court Rule 1:40, serving as an independent mediator and Early Settlement Panelist in both Union and Essex Counties.

Seiden has been recognized as a Best Lawyer in America every year since 2015 and named to Super Lawyers in New Jersey since 2015, including repeated recognition among the state's Top 100 Lawyers and Top 50 Female Lawyers. A trustee of the New Jersey State Bar Association since 2021, she previously chaired the Association's Family Law Section Executive Committee and has been a member of that committee since 2008. Seiden earned her J.D. magna cum laude from New York Law School in 1996, where she served as managing editor of the Law Review, and her B.A. in Justice, cum laude, from American University in 1993.
attorneys.meta_description: Sheryl J. Seiden is an AV Preeminent, AAML-NJ Fellow leading SeidenFreed LLC's family law practice in Cranford and Union County, NJ.

attorneys.biography:
Gary N. Skoloff is a founding partner of Skoloff & Wolfe, P.C. in Livingston, where for more than six decades he has represented executives, business owners, professional athletes, and other high-net-worth individuals in complex, high-profile New Jersey matrimonial matters, including the landmark Baby M surrogacy case. Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney and a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, he holds an AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell and is the author of New Jersey Family Law Practice, now in its fifteenth edition and widely used by judges and practitioners throughout the state.

A past Chair of the American Bar Association's Family Law Section and past President of the AAML's New Jersey Chapter, Skoloff also chaired the Family Law Section of the New Jersey State Bar Association and served on the New Jersey Supreme Court's Family Law Committee. He has been recognized in The Best Lawyers in America since 1983 and selected to New Jersey Super Lawyers for more than twenty consecutive years, and has received the NJSBA Family Law Section's Saul A. Tischler Award, the Trial Attorneys of New Jersey's Trial Bar Award, and NJICLE's Clapp Award. Skoloff earned his J.D. from Rutgers Law School and his B.A. from Rutgers University, and has taught as an adjunct professor of Family Law at Seton Hall and Rutgers law schools.
attorneys.meta_description: Livingston-based AAML Fellow and author of New Jersey Family Law Practice, guiding Essex County clients through complex, high-net-worth divorces.

attorneys.biography:
Lynne Strober is a Shareholder and Co-Chair of the Matrimonial and Family Law Practice Group at Mandelbaum Barrett PC in Roseland, where for more than 45 years she has advised clients on complex equitable distribution, alimony, child support, custody and parenting time, prenuptial agreements, and post-judgment matters. Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney and a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers' New Jersey Chapter, she holds an AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell and has been recognized in The Best Lawyers in America since 2019 and on the New Jersey Super Lawyers list for over twenty years.

Strober is President-Elect of the AAML's New Jersey Chapter and a past Chair of both the New Jersey State Bar Association's Family Law Section (1992-1993) and the Essex County Bar Association's Family Law Executive Committee (1988-1991). Earlier in her career she was appointed by the Chief Justice of the New Jersey Supreme Court to chair the Matrimonial Certification Committee and serve on the Board on Attorney Certification. Named a “Family Law Trailblazer” by the National Law Journal, she earned her J.D. from the University of New Hampshire Franklin Pierce School of Law and her B.A. from Syracuse University.
attorneys.meta_description: Roseland-based AAML Fellow and 20-year Super Lawyer guiding Essex County families through complex, high-net-worth matrimonial matters.

attorneys.biography:
Noel S. Tonneman is a partner at Tonneman & Connors, LLC in Little Silver, where she has practiced exclusively in matrimonial law since 1988, focusing primarily on Monmouth and Ocean County clients. Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney and a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, she currently serves as Second Vice President of the AAML's New Jersey Chapter and is certified by the AAML as a Family Law Arbitrator. She has been recognized in The Best Lawyers in America since 2006 and on the New Jersey Super Lawyers list since 2005.

Before founding Tonneman & Connors, Tonneman spent 20 years in the Family Law Department at Wilentz, Goldman & Spitzer and later co-founded Tonneman, Vuotto, Enis & White. She has served as Trustee of the Monmouth Bar Association and Chair of its Family Law Committee, is Treasurer of the Monmouth Bar Foundation, and is Secretary of the New Jersey Divorce Arbitrators Association. A former member of the New Jersey Supreme Court's Family Law Practice Committee, she earned her J.D. from Rutgers School of Law-Newark and her B.A., cum laude, from Bryn Mawr College.
attorneys.meta_description: Little Silver matrimonial attorney and AAML Fellow with 35+ years representing Monmouth and Ocean County families in divorce and custody matters.

attorneys.biography:
Paul H. Townsend is a founding partner of Townsend, Tomaio, Newmark & Clancy, LLC in Whippany, where he has practiced family and divorce law for four decades. Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney — a distinction held by only about two percent of his peers — he has been selected to the New Jersey Super Lawyers list every year since 2005 and recognized in The Best Lawyers in America. His practice draws on personal experience with divorce, which he brings to his honest, direct, and compassionate approach to representing clients through high-conflict litigation, mediation, and post-judgment matters.

Townsend is a member of the Morris County Bar Association and previously served the Union County and Somerset County Bar Associations. He successfully argued Bisbing v. Bisbing before the New Jersey Supreme Court, establishing a more child-focused standard of review in relocation cases, and has lectured on family law topics for the Institute for Continuing Legal Education and Seton Hall University. He earned his J.D. from Ohio Northern University's Claude W. Pettit College of Law and his B.A. in Political Science from Albright College.
attorneys.meta_description: Whippany, NJ Certified Matrimonial Attorney and founding partner with 40 years handling high-conflict Morris County divorce and custody cases.

attorneys.biography:
Amanda S. Trigg leads the Family Law practice at Cohn Lifland Pearlman Herrmann & Knopf LLP in Saddle Brook, where for more than 30 years she has represented entrepreneurs, physicians, and other high-net-worth clients in complex litigation, arbitration, and mediation. Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney, she is ranked Band 3 in Chambers High Net Worth 2025 for Family/Matrimonial law and holds an AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell. She has been named to The Best Lawyers in America since 2015, including multiple “Lawyer of the Year” honors, and to the New Jersey Super Lawyers list since 2011.

A national leader in her field, Trigg is Vice President of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, a Past President of its New Jersey Chapter, and a former Chair of the New Jersey State Bar Association's Family Law Section. She is a Fellow of the International Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers and teaches annually at the National Family Law Trial Institute in Houston. She earned her J.D. from Emory University School of Law and her B.A., cum laude, from Brandeis University, where she was a Justice Louis D. Brandeis Scholar.
attorneys.meta_description: Bergen County Chambers-ranked matrimonial attorney handling high-net-worth divorces for entrepreneurs and professionals in Saddle Brook, NJ.

attorneys.biography:
Charles F. Vuotto, Jr. is Of Counsel in the Family Law Practice Group at A.Y. Strauss in Livingston, where he handles financially intricate divorce matters involving business valuation, executive compensation, and stock options for high-net-worth clients. Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney and a Fellow of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers, he holds an AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell and has been recognized in The Best Lawyers in America since 2009 and on the New Jersey Super Lawyers list since 2005.

A past Chair of the New Jersey State Bar Association's Family Law Section and its Editor-in-Chief of the New Jersey Family Lawyer for fourteen years, Vuotto received the NJSBA Family Law Section's 2016 Saul Tischler Award for lifetime achievement. He previously co-founded Tonneman, Vuotto, Enis & White and served on the New Jersey Supreme Court's Family Part Practice Committee and its Ad Hoc Committee on the Arbitration of Family Matters. He earned his J.D. from Ohio Northern University's Claude W. Pettit College of Law and his B.A. from Seton Hall University.
attorneys.meta_description: Livingston, NJ matrimonial attorney and Saul Tischler Award winner handling complex, high-net-worth divorces involving business valuation.

attorneys.biography:
Salvatore A. Simeone is a partner at Weinberger Divorce & Family Law Group, LLC in Parsippany, New Jersey, where he has practiced family law exclusively since 1994. Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Attorney since 2011, Mr. Simeone focuses his practice on high-conflict divorce and custody litigation, custodial parent relocation, domestic violence matters, alimony, child support, and complex business valuations in equitable distribution. He practices throughout Morris, Sussex, Essex, Somerset, Union, and Bergen Counties and is frequently appointed by the New Jersey courts as a Guardian ad Litem and Parenting Coordinator.

A graduate of Seton Hall University School of Law, Mr. Simeone has been named to the New Jersey Super Lawyers list every year since 2005. He is a former Executive Committee member of the American Academy of Matrimonial Lawyers and served as a Barrister with the NJ Inn of the Court's North Jersey section. He is a member of the New Jersey State Bar Association and the Morris County Bar Association, and has volunteered with the Morris County Battered Women Legal Advocacy Program.
attorneys.meta_description: NJ Certified Matrimonial Attorney in Morris County since 1994, focused on high-conflict divorce, custody, and complex asset valuations.

attorneys.biography:
Bari Zell Weinberger is the founder and Managing Partner of Weinberger Divorce & Family Law Group, LLC, a New Jersey firm devoted exclusively to divorce and family law. Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Attorney, she represents clients across the state in divorce, child custody, child support, alimony, and equitable distribution matters, and is trained in both mediation and collaborative divorce.

A graduate of Suffolk University Law School, Ms. Weinberger holds an AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell and has been recognized by Best Lawyers in America since 2019 and New Jersey Super Lawyers since 2015. She has served as a board member and Master of the New Jersey Family Inns of Court and is active in the New Jersey State Bar Association, the American Bar Association, and the Morris County Bar Association. Her commentary on family law has appeared in the Wall Street Journal, Forbes, and the New Jersey Law Journal.
attorneys.meta_description: Bari Weinberger, AV Preeminent NJ Certified Matrimonial Attorney, leads a statewide divorce and family law practice based in Mount Laurel.

attorneys.biography:
Tanya N. Helfand is the Managing Partner of the Morristown office of Sarno da Costa D'Aniello Maceri Webb LLC, where she leads the firm's Family and Matrimonial Law Division. A Certified Matrimonial Attorney by the Supreme Court of New Jersey and a Certified Divorce Financial Analyst, she represents clients with substantial marital estates, including business owners and executives, in divorce, custody, alimony, and complex equitable distribution matters throughout Essex, Morris, Bergen, Somerset, and Union Counties.

Ms. Helfand earned her J.D. from Pace University School of Law and her B.A. from Union College. She has been selected to the New Jersey Super Lawyers list every year since 2017 and was named a Top Lawyer by Morris County magazine for 2024-2025. She is a longtime member of the Morris and Essex County Early Settlement Panels, serves on the board of the Jewish Law Symposium, and is licensed to practice in both New Jersey and New York.
attorneys.meta_description: Certified Matrimonial Attorney managing the Morristown office, handling complex financial divorces for Morris and Essex County families.

attorneys.biography:
Carolann M. Aschoff is the founder of Carolann M. Aschoff, P.C., a family law firm with offices in Bayonne, Jersey City, and Livingston, New Jersey. Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Attorney, a distinction held by fewer than two percent of the state's lawyers, she has focused her practice exclusively on divorce, custody, support, and mediation matters for over 30 years, and regularly serves as a court-appointed guardian and parenting coordinator.

A graduate of Seton Hall University School of Law, Ms. Aschoff was named Matrimonial Attorney of the Year by the Hudson County Bar Association in 2002 and has chaired the Hudson County Early Settlement Panel Committee for more than two decades. She is a longtime member of the Hudson County, Essex County, and New Jersey State Bar Associations and previously served on the New Jersey State Ethics Committee for District 6.
attorneys.meta_description: Hudson County Certified Matrimonial Attorney with 30+ years handling divorce and custody cases from her Bayonne, Jersey City, and Livingston offices.

attorneys.biography:
Harriet E. Raghnal is a partner at Raghnal Burroughs, LLC in Livingston, New Jersey, where she focuses exclusively on divorce, child custody, child support, and spousal support matters. A Supreme Court Certified Matrimonial Attorney with more than 19 years of experience, she brings both legal expertise and cultural sensitivity to representing Essex County families through some of life's most difficult transitions.

Ms. Raghnal earned her J.D. from Rutgers University School of Law and her B.A. from Long Island University/C.W. Post. She is a member of the Barry Croland Family Law American Inn of Court, the Essex County Bar Association, and the New Jersey State Bar Association's Family Part, and previously served as First Vice President of the Jamaican American Bar Association, North East. She is also active in numerous Essex County community organizations.
attorneys.meta_description: Supreme Court Certified Matrimonial Attorney in Livingston, NJ with 19+ years handling Essex County divorce and custody cases.

attorneys.biography:
Lori Cieckiewicz is a partner at Weiner Law Group LLP, practicing from the firm's Parsippany, New Jersey office. A Certified Matrimonial Law Attorney with more than 28 years of experience, she handles all areas of divorce and family law litigation and mediation, and is frequently appointed as a Parent Coordinator in high-conflict custody matters throughout Morris County and beyond.

A graduate of Rutgers School of Law-Newark, Ms. Cieckiewicz has been recognized by Best Lawyers in America since 2025 and was named Hudson County Bar Association's Family Lawyer of the Year in 2011, having previously served as the association's President. She is a Rule 1:40 qualified family law mediator and trained collaborative law attorney, and is active in the New Jersey State Bar Association's Family Law Committee.
attorneys.meta_description: Best Lawyers-recognized Certified Matrimonial Attorney in Parsippany, NJ with 28+ years of divorce and family law litigation experience.

attorneys.biography:
Stephanie "Steph" Lomurro is the managing partner of Manzi Epstein Lomurro & DeCataldo, LLC (MELD Law) in West Orange, where she has practiced family law exclusively since 2009. Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney, she represents clients in divorce, custody and parenting-time disputes, complex asset division, domestic violence matters, and adoptions, and also serves as a qualified Rule 1:40 mediator and court-appointed parenting coordinator.

A New York Law School graduate who clerked for the Family Part in Essex County, Lomurro was named to the New Jersey Super Lawyers list for 2025-2026 after more than a decade on the Rising Stars list, and has been recognized in Best Lawyers for Family Law and Family Law Mediation since 2022. She is active on the NJSBA Family Law Executive Committee and the Hudson County Bar Association, and clients consistently praise her calm, strategic approach to high-conflict cases.
attorneys.meta_description: Stephanie Lomurro is a Certified Matrimonial Attorney and managing partner of MELD Law in West Orange, NJ, handling divorce and family law cases.

attorneys.biography:
Thomas "Tom" DeCataldo is a partner at Manzi Epstein Lomurro & DeCataldo, LLC (MELD Law) in West Orange, where he has focused exclusively on family law since beginning his career as a law clerk to the Family Part in Essex County. He is certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Attorney and represents business owners, executives, and other professionals in custody, support, equitable distribution, and prenuptial agreement matters across Essex, Union, Morris, and surrounding counties.

DeCataldo has litigated cases through the Appellate Division and Supreme Court of New Jersey while also regularly pursuing mediation and arbitration as alternatives to trial. He has been recognized in Best Lawyers for Family Law since 2022 and named to the New Jersey Super Lawyers list for 2025-2026 after several years on the Rising Stars list, and is a frequent lecturer for the New Jersey State Bar Association on family law topics.
attorneys.meta_description: Thomas DeCataldo is a Certified Matrimonial Attorney and partner at MELD Law in West Orange, NJ, focused exclusively on family law matters.

attorneys.biography:
Patricia Garity Smits is the founder and managing member of the Law Offices of Patricia Garity Smits, L.L.C. in Lebanon, where she has practiced family law since her 1978 admission to the New Jersey bar. A Certified Matrimonial Trial Attorney, she handles divorce, custody, premarital agreements, and property settlement matters ranging from mediation and uncontested dissolutions to complex, high-conflict litigation.

Smits has served on numerous state and national bar committees, including as Vice Chair of the New Jersey Supreme Court Family Part Practice Committee and on the American Bar Association's Family Law Section governing council, and is a frequent lecturer and published author on family law topics. She also serves as an Early Settlement Panelist in Morris County.
attorneys.meta_description: Patricia Garity Smits is a Certified Matrimonial Trial Attorney in Lebanon, NJ, handling divorce, custody, and family law since 1978.

attorneys.biography:
Gina M. Sorge is an attorney with Javerbaum Wurgaft Hicks Kahn Wikstrom & Sinins, P.C. in Roseland, where she practices in the Chancery Division/Family Part alongside products liability and complex commercial litigation matters. Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as both a Civil Trial Attorney and a Matrimonial Law Attorney, she has represented clients in palimony, relocation, and high-conflict custody disputes.

A University of Miami School of Law graduate admitted to practice in 1986, Sorge serves as a court-appointed panelist for the Early Settlement Program in Morris, Bergen, and Essex counties and was inducted as a Fellow of the Litigation Counsel of America in 2022. She joined Javerbaum Wurgaft following the firm's 2026 combination with Lum, Drasco & Positan.
attorneys.meta_description: Gina Sorge is a Certified Matrimonial and Civil Trial Attorney at Javerbaum Wurgaft in Roseland, NJ, handling family and civil litigation.

attorneys.biography:
Robin C. Bogan is a partner at SeidenFreed LLC, practicing family law across northern and central New Jersey since her 1996 admission to the bar. Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney, she represents clients in divorce, custody, and support matters and also serves as a mediator, drawing on nearly three decades of trial experience.

Bogan has been named to the New Jersey Super Lawyers list every year since 2012 and was included among the state's Top 100 Lawyers and Top 50 Women Lawyers. A former Chair of the NJSBA Family Law Executive Committee and current NJSBA Trustee for Morris County, she was a founding partner of Pallarino & Bogan, LLP for 21 years before joining SeidenFreed.
attorneys.meta_description: Robin Bogan is a Certified Matrimonial Attorney and partner at SeidenFreed LLC, handling divorce and family law across northern New Jersey.

attorneys.biography:
Melinda L. Singer is a solo practitioner in Hackensack who has devoted her practice to family law for over 40 years since her 1985 admission to the New Jersey bar. Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney, she represents clients in divorce, custody, support, and domestic violence matters, and serves as a court-approved mediator for both economic and domestic violence family law cases.

Singer has been named to the New Jersey Super Lawyers list annually since 2017 and was recognized by (201) Magazine as a Top Lawyer in Matrimonial and Family Law. She serves on the NJ Supreme Court's Family Law Practice Committee and has provided legal commentary for national television networks including truTV, Fox, and MSNBC.
attorneys.meta_description: Melinda Singer is a Certified Matrimonial Attorney and solo practitioner in Hackensack, NJ, with over 40 years of family law experience.

attorneys.biography:
David Scott Carton is a Partner and Co-Chair of the Matrimonial and Family Law practice group at Mandelbaum Barrett PC in Roseland, New Jersey. Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney since 2007, he has practiced family law since 1995, representing clients across the full range of divorce and family law matters -- including prenuptial agreements, custody and parenting time, child support, alimony, domestic violence, and complex asset division -- with a particular focus on ultra-high-net-worth divorce. He is also a court-qualified mediator and Blue-Ribbon Early Settlement Panelist who is frequently selected by judges and peers to mediate high-conflict economic and custody disputes.

Carton has been recognized by Best Lawyers in America for Family Law every year since 2020 and has been selected to the New Jersey Super Lawyers list continuously from 2020 through 2026, following an earlier Super Lawyers Rising Stars selection in 2009. He received the Essex County Bar Association's Family Law Practice Award in 2019 and remains active in the New Jersey State Bar Association, the Essex County Bar Association -- where he serves on the Family Law Executive Committee and co-chairs the Family Bench Bar Committee -- and the Middlesex County Bar Association. He earned his J.D. from New England School of Law, where he served as Managing Business Editor of the Law Journal, and his B.A. from American University.
attorneys.meta_description: David Scott Carton is a Certified Matrimonial Law Attorney and Super Lawyer serving Essex County, NJ from Mandelbaum Barrett's Roseland office.

attorneys.biography:
Meridith J. Bronson is Counsel in the Family Law Department at Greenbaum, Rowe, Smith & Davis LLP in Roseland, New Jersey, where she has practiced family law for more than three decades. Certified by the Supreme Court of New Jersey as a Matrimonial Law Attorney, she concentrates her practice on complex divorce and family law matters, including custody, alimony and child support, prenuptial and cohabitation agreements, mediation, and post-judgment proceedings. Her deep understanding of financial issues in matrimonial cases -- ranging from privately held businesses and business valuations to stock options, deferred compensation, and retirement benefits -- has made her a trusted advisor in high-profile and financially complex matters.

Bronson is a Martindale-Hubbell AV Preeminent-rated attorney and was named to New Jersey Super Lawyers in the Family Law practice area from 2005 through 2011. She belongs to the Family Law Sections of the New Jersey State Bar Association and the American Bar Association, as well as the Essex County Bar Association's Family Law Committee, and serves as a Master with the Barry Croland Family Law American Inn of Court. She holds a J.D. from Seton Hall University School of Law (1984) and a B.A., magna cum laude and Phi Beta Kappa, from Drew University (1980).
attorneys.meta_description: Meridith Bronson is a NJ Certified Matrimonial Attorney at Greenbaum Rowe in Roseland, Essex County, focused on complex divorce and family law matters.

attorneys.biography:
Sheryl E. Koomer is a partner at Hellring Lindeman Goldstein & Siegal LLP in Roseland, New Jersey, where she concentrates her practice in family law and appellate practice. She is certified as a Matrimonial Attorney by the Supreme Court of New Jersey, a distinction she earned in 2006, and has been licensed to practice in New Jersey since 1988.

A 1988 graduate of Rutgers University School of Law in Newark, Koomer began her legal career as a law clerk to the Hon. Richard S. Rebeck, J.S.C., in the Family Part of the Superior Court of New Jersey. She has served for decades as a panelist for the Union County Early Settlement Program and is a member of the New Jersey Bar Association. Her professional recognition includes multiple years of selection to the New Jersey Super Lawyers list for family law. Koomer earned her undergraduate degree, magna cum laude, from Brandeis University in 1985.
attorneys.meta_description: Sheryl Koomer is a Certified Matrimonial Attorney and partner at Hellring Lindeman Goldstein & Siegal in Roseland, Essex County, NJ.

attorneys.biography:
Lynn Fontaine Newsome has practiced family law exclusively since her admission to the New Jersey bar in 1981. Now a Member at Lite DePalma Greenberg & Afanador, LLC, where she chairs the firm's Family Law team, she is a Certified Matrimonial Attorney handling divorce, custody, palimony, prenuptial agreements, civil unions, appeals, and domestic violence matters. An experienced litigator, she also serves as a certified mediator and arbitrator and regularly accepts court appointments as a discovery master and guardian.

A Past President of the New Jersey State Bar Association and former Chair of its Family Law Section, Newsome has also served as President of the Morris County Bar Association and is a longstanding member of the New Jersey Supreme Court Family Law Committee. She has been selected to Super Lawyers every year since 2005 and named to Best Lawyers in America for Family Law since 2015, and holds an AV Preeminent rating from Martindale-Hubbell. She earned her B.A. from Seton Hall University in 1977 and her J.D. from Seton Hall University School of Law in 1981.
attorneys.meta_description: Certified Matrimonial Attorney Lynn Fontaine Newsome has handled NJ divorce and family law cases in Newark and Essex County since 1981.

attorneys.biography:
Stacey A. Cozewith is a Member of Saiber LLC and Chair of the firm's Family Law practice group, based in the firm's Madison, New Jersey office. With more than two decades of experience, she represents clients across the full spectrum of family law matters, including divorce, prenuptial agreements, custody and support, alimony, equitable distribution, domestic violence, relocation, and civil union dissolution. She is among roughly two percent of New Jersey family law attorneys certified by the state Supreme Court as a Matrimonial Attorney, and she also serves as a certified mediator and arbitrator in complex family law disputes.

Cozewith has been recognized in Best Lawyers in America for Family Law since 2020 and named to New Jersey Super Lawyers every year from 2021 through 2026, including the 2025-2026 Top 50 Women list. Before joining Saiber in January 2025, she spent nearly two decades at a boutique New Jersey family law firm and began her legal career as a law clerk to the Hon. Thomas H. Dilts, P.J.F.P., in Somerset County. She earned her J.D. from Rutgers Law School-Newark in 2004 and her B.A. from Johns Hopkins University in 2001.
attorneys.meta_description: Stacey Cozewith is a certified matrimonial attorney and Family Law Chair at Saiber LLC in Madison, NJ, serving Morris County families.

attorneys.biography:
Steven J. Martino is a Certified Matrimonial Trial Attorney under the Supreme Court of New Jersey and a Certified Family Law Mediator with over 37 years of legal experience. A Partner at PEM Law LLP in West Orange, he leads the firm's Family Law practice, concentrating on divorce, mediation, and complex matrimonial and family-related matters, while also maintaining a practice in municipal law, real estate, and civil litigation. He has served on New Jersey's Matrimonial Early Settlement Panel for over 30 years and is frequently appointed by judges as guardian ad litem in difficult custody cases.

Martino has been recognized by Super Lawyers annually from 2012 through 2026 for his family law work. He earned his J.D. from Seton Hall University School of Law in 1987 and his B.A. in Economics from Drew University in 1984, and has been a member of the New Jersey State Bar Association and Essex County Bar Association since 1987.
attorneys.meta_description: Steven J. Martino, a Certified Matrimonial Attorney at PEM Law LLP in West Orange, NJ, brings over 37 years of family law and mediation experience.
